name: Money Market Pawn Shop
description_short: Money Market Pawn Shop is a Portland-based pawn lender offering 90-day collateral loans, buying, and selling of jewelry, firearms, electronics, tools, and musical instruments.
description_long:
Money Market Pawn Shop has operated in Portland, Oregon since 2000, establishing itself as a collateral-based lending business with over 23 years of operational history. The shop is located at 13607 SE Powell Boulevard and specializes in short-term pawn loans secured by personal items of value. The business positions itself as a respectful alternative to traditional lending, emphasizing courteous service and knowledgeable staff who bring over 16 years of combined pawn industry experience.

The company offers three primary service categories: pawn loans, buying, and selling. Their core product is 90-day collateral loans with unlimited renewal options, allowing borrowers to extend terms as needed. They accept a broad range of collateral including jewelry, gold, firearms, electronics, tools, stereos, generators, digital cameras, musical instruments (guitars and amplifiers), sporting goods, and other merchandise. The business also operates as a secondary market retailer, buying unused items from customers and selling inventory to the public at discounted prices. They maintain an FFL (Federal Firearms License) for gun transfers, indicating regulatory compliance in firearms transactions.

Money Market Pawn Shop differentiates itself through its emphasis on customer respect and accessibility. The website highlights courteous service, no-pressure transactions, and a wide selection of gift merchandise. They offer occasional promotional discounts (15% off musical equipment and electronics/tools noted on their site). The business maintains multiple communication channels including phone, text, and email for different service inquiries, suggesting operational sophistication. Their website includes accessibility features and clear privacy policies.

The primary limitation is the lack of detailed information about loan terms, interest rates, or fee structures on the website. While they advertise 90-day loans and renewals, specific APR, maximum loan amounts, redemption terms, and default consequences are not disclosed. The business operates with standard pawn shop economics—customers must forfeit collateral if loans aren't repaid, making this unsuitable for those unable to reclaim items. Like all pawn operations, this represents a relatively expensive short-term financing option compared to traditional lenders, though potentially faster and less documentation-intensive.
pros: 90-day loan terms with unlimited renewal options, providing flexibility beyond single-term structures
Broad collateral acceptance including jewelry, electronics, tools, firearms, and musical instruments
Licensed FFL dealer for compliant firearms transfers and transactions
Multiple contact channels (phone, text, email) with specialized departments for loan vs. firearms questions
Established 23+ year operating history with 16+ years combined staff pawn experience
Buy and sell services allowing customers to liquidate unused items for cash immediately
Periodic promotional discounts (15% off music equipment and electronics/tools)
Accessible website with commitment to ADA compliance and privacy protection
cons: No interest rates, APR, fee structures, or maximum loan amounts disclosed on website
Collateral forfeiture upon non-repayment—higher cost than traditional loans, typically much higher interest than credit alternatives
Limited operational transparency regarding loan approval criteria, valuation methods, or underwriting process
Website 'About' page returns 404 error, reducing available company information
No online application or quote tool; requires in-person visit or phone inquiry for loan terms
best_for: Individuals needing quick cash ($500-$10,000+) who own valuable items but lack traditional credit
People seeking collateral loans without credit checks or extensive documentation requirements
Customers buying used jewelry, electronics, musical instruments, or firearms at secondary market prices
Gun owners needing FFL transfer services or those seeking licensed firearm transactions
diagnosis: Money Market Pawn Shop is best for borrowers needing immediate cash secured by collateral who lack traditional credit access or prefer faster approval than banks. The primary caveat is that pawn loans are expensive compared to personal loans or credit alternatives, and defaulted loans result in permanent loss of the pawned item—this is viable only for customers who can realistically repay within 90 days or afford to lose the collateral.
services: Jewelry and gold buying and lending
Firearm pawn loans and sales with FFL licensing
General merchandise buying (customers selling unused items)
Retail sales of pawned and consigned inventory
Gift merchandise sales (jewelry, sporting goods, electronics)
